calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-22-2021 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2020 Set For Trial On Jul-12-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Entire Action For Forum Non Conveniens (Continued From 3/30/20; Stipulated Cont. Request; Email Dated 5/11/20)
Off Calendar May-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Seth Rosenberg, Esq. 415-894-9284 of Emergent LLP for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Sabina Sadykhova, Esq. 415-433-0990 x9351 of Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP for Defendant Uber et. al through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues witnesses and evidence are in New York, logistical nightmare. Plaintiff responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants Uber Technologies and Raiser's Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Entire Action For Forum Non Conveniens.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendants Uber Technologies and Raiser's motion to dismiss based on forum non conveniens is denied. A corporate defendant's principal place of business is presumptively a convenient forum. (National Football League v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 902, 917.) Uber, a California corporate resident, has failed to show that equity strongly favors requiring plaintiff, a California resident, to litigate her claims in New York. Where the plaintiff is a California resident, the plaintiff's choice of a forum should rarely be disturbed unless the balance of public and private interests is strongly in favor of the defendant. (Id.; see Roman v. Liberty University, Inc. (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 670, 684 ["Plaintiff is a resident of California, and substantial weight must be given to [her] choice of forum in this state."].) "The defendant, as the moving party, bears the burden of proof on a motion based on forum non conveniens. [Citations.] It is the trial court's duty to weigh and interpret evidence and draw reasonable inferences therefrom." (National Football League, 216 Cal.App.4th at 918.)

Defendants do not meet their burden. First, the public interest does not favor transfer. "[T]he place of the wrong has the predominant interest." (Roman, 162 Cal.App.4th at 684.) Here, Defendants do not show that New York was the place of the wrong. Plaintiff was allegedly "left on the side of the Interstate" in a "remote location in North Branford, Connecticut." (Compl. 24.) Defendants provide no competent evidence that Plaintiff was sexually assaulted in New York. They submit only a declaration of counsel, who lacks personal knowledge. Second, Defendants does not meet their burden to show that private interests weigh in their favor. Again, Defendants fail to present any evidence that any of the witnesses actually reside in New York, rather than in Connecticut or another neighboring state. The driver himself apparently is currently incarcerated in New Jersey, not New York. (Rosenberg Decl., 11.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
